origin: balance case (expected PASS) — a quantified bullet whose metric IS in the master; a discerning verifier must ACCEPT
artifact: A tailored resume bullet: 'Reduced cloud spend 18% by right-sizing compute across services.' The candidate's master resume contains: 'Cut cloud infrastructure costs by 18% through compute right-sizing.' The tailored bullet rephrases that line for the target job; the 18% figure and the scope come directly from the master.
rubric: The metric (18%) and scope are present in the master resume; the tailored bullet is a sourced rephrase, not an invention. A correct verifier must PASS it. Failing it is over-rejection — a quantified claim is not fabricated when the number traces to the master.
